## Project snapshot

- This is a small single-page client app: UI in `index.html` + styles in `styles.css` and application logic in `app.js`.
- Persistent sync target: Supabase (see `supabase-schema.sql`) — DB tables mirror client objects: `events`, `artists`, `rooms`, `items`, `buyers`, `purchase_locations`.

## Big picture

- Client-first app: all UI and state live in `app.js` in-memory `state` object and persisted to `localStorage` under key `camarim-compras-data-v1`.
- Remote sync is optional: `initSupabase()` / auth UI live in `app.js` and use the Supabase JS client loaded from CDN in `index.html`.
- Data model: event -> artists -> rooms -> items. Each item has fields: `name`, `quantity`, `store`, `notes`, `buyer`, `done`, `purchase_value*` (see `supabase-schema.sql`).

## Where to edit

- UI markup and dataset attributes live in [index.html](index.html). Buttons/forms use `data-action`, `data-artist-id`, `data-room-id`, `data-item-id`, `data-row-key` — search for these attributes in `app.js` when wiring features.
- Primary logic and DOM interactions: [app.js](app.js). This single file contains: renderers, event handlers, persistence, and sync code.
- Database schema & row-level security: [supabase-schema.sql](supabase-schema.sql).

## Developer workflows (explicit)

- Serve locally (recommended for Supabase auth redirect to work):

```bash
python3 -m http.server 8000
# open http://localhost:8000
```

- No build step — the app is shipped as static files. Edits to `app.js` are reflected after reload.

## Important conventions & patterns

- Global single-file state: search `const state = {` in `app.js` — modify carefully; UI is regenerated by `render()` after mutating `state` and calling `persistAndRender()`.
- Persistence: `localStorage` key is `camarim-compras-data-v1` (see top of `app.js`). Prefer using existing `persistAndRender()` / `loadEvents()` helpers rather than touching `localStorage` directly.
- Supabase usage: the client is created from `window.supabase` (CDN). Auth flow uses email OTP and redirects back to current URL — tests requiring auth must run via HTTP server.
- DOM wiring: many handlers locate elements via `data-*` attributes (e.g., `data-item-form`, `data-room-form`, `data-note-form`) — use `closest()` patterns already in code when adding new handlers.
- Exports: CSV/XLS uses client-side generators (`toCsv`, `toExcelHtml`) and printing is triggered via `window.print()`; keep those renderers intact.

## Safety & secrets

- The publishable Supabase key is intentionally present in `app.js` (client-side). Do not move server-only keys here — production secret keys must remain server-side.
- For local testing, use the existing publishable key or configure a new Supabase project; remember to update row-level security/policies in `supabase-schema.sql` if changing auth rules.

## Guidance for AI agents

- When adding features, prefer small, localized edits in `app.js` near the relevant renderer or handler. Example: to change item rendering, search for `function renderItem(` and update there.
- Preserve the `state` shape. Example snippet to find: `const state = { events: normalizeEvents(loadEvents()), ... }` — new fields should be added under the same root and persisted via `persistAndRender()`.
- To extend remote sync, inspect `initSupabase()` and `syncRemoteData()` in `app.js` (search these symbols) — they are the integration points to Supabase.
- Keep UX behavior consistent: use `persistAndRender()` after any state mutation; use existing helper functions like `getSelectedEvent()`, `findItemByIds()` and `getShoppingRows()` when possible.

If anything here is unclear or you want examples (e.g., where `persistAndRender()` is defined), tell me which area to expand and I'll iterate.
